perturbation.original_text: 3. Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sig n a Non -Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees that it wil l take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient may disclose any Confidential Information to third parties at their sole discretion, regardless of whether those third parties have signed a Non-Disclosure Agreement. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly limits disclosure to specific individuals with a need-to-know and who have signed a similar NDA. The change grants the Recipient broad discretion to disclose to *any* third party, even without an NDA. This directly contradicts the earlier requirements for controlled disclosure, creating uncertainty about the confidentiality obligations. This change creates an in-text contradiction because the requirement to notify the Discloser is undermined if the Recipient can disclose information freely, potentially leading to the Discloser not being informed of disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient may disclose any Confidential Information to third parties at their sole discretion, regardless of whether those third parties have signed a Non-Disclosure Agreement. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text provides the Recipient with the discretion to disclose Confidential Information to third parties 'regardless of whether those third parties have signed a Non-Disclosure Agreement'. This directly contradicts the requirement in the original text to limit disclosure and have third parties sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement, creating an in-text contradiction within section 3 itself (which is also the contradicted text). The requirement to notify the Discloser is undermined, as the Recipient can disclose freely, potentially leading to the Discloser not being informed of all disclosures. This falls under the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' category because the change creates conflicting implications within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Term. T he foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue for a period of three (3) years following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Term. The foregoing commitments will terminate immediately upon the termination of discussions between the parties. However, any misuse of confidential information during the term of the agreement will be subject to legal action indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause establishes a fixed term of three years for the confidentiality obligations. The modification states that the commitments end immediately upon termination of discussions. This creates a direct conflict regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligations. The added statement about indefinite legal action for misuse does not clarify this contradiction, as it doesn't specify whether the confidential information is still protected *after* the discussion termination - creating further in-text contradiction and uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Term. The foregoing commitments will terminate immediately upon the termination of discussions between the parties. However, any misuse of confidential information during the term of the agreement will be subject to legal action indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a 3-year term for confidentiality, whereas the changed text states that the commitments terminate immediately upon termination of discussions. The added statement about indefinite legal action doesn't resolve this contradiction and the Contradicted text is identical to the Change Text, creating uncertainty about whether confidential information is protected after the discussion termination. This creates a direct conflict and makes enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall be binding upon and for the benefit o f the undersigned parties, their successors and assigns, provided that Confidential Information may not be assigned without the prior written consent of the Discloser. Failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall not constitute a waiver of any term hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall be binding upon and for the benefit o f the undersigned parties only. Confidential Information may be freely assigned by the Recipient to any third party, without the need for consent from the Discloser. Failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of that term, and any subsequent attempt to enforce it will be deemed invalid.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause prohibits the assignment of Confidential Information without the Discloser's consent. The modified clause permits free assignment by the Recipient, directly contradicting the original restriction. The original statement says that failure to enforce, does not constitute a wavier of any term, while the modified text states that failure to enforce shall constitute a waiver of the term. Making this an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall be binding upon and for the benefit o f the undersigned parties only. Confidential Information may be freely assigned by the Recipient to any third party, without the need for consent from the Discloser. Failure to enforce any provision of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of that term, and any subsequent attempt to enforce it will be deemed invalid.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8 introduces a direct contradiction regarding the assignment of Confidential Information and the waiver of terms. The original text prohibits the assignment of confidential information without consent, while the changed text permits free assignment. Also, the original text states that failure to enforce doesn't constitute a waiver, whereas the changed text states that it does constitute a waiver. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text here, highlights that these changes cause a strong, direct contradiction by altering the conditions and enforceability of the agreement, thus a 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Re medies. Recipient agrees that its obligations hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covena nts and agreements set forth herein. Accordingly, Recipient agrees and acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Remedies. The Recipient does not agree that its obligations hereunder are necessary or reasonable to protect the Discloser. Monetary damages are deemed adequate compensation for any breach. The Recipient does not acknowledge that a breach will cause irreparable harm to the Discloser, and injunctive relief is not available.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause asserts that monetary damages are inadequate, justifying injunctive relief. The modified clause states that monetary damages *are* adequate and injunctive relief is *not* available. This creates a direct conflict about the available remedies in case of a breach. This is an in-text contradiction because it directly negates the Discloser's ability to seek an injunction, a statement previously agreed upon in the original agreement, while at the same time being in the agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Remedies. The Recipient does not agree that its obligations hereunder are necessary or reasonable to protect the Discloser. Monetary damages are deemed adequate compensation for any breach. The Recipient does not acknowledge that a breach will cause irreparable harm to the Discloser, and injunctive relief is not available.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the remedies clause. The original text states that monetary damages are inadequate and injunctive relief is available. The changed text explicitly states the opposite: monetary damages are adequate, and injunctive relief is not available. The contradicted text is the exact same as the changed text, further reinforcing that there is now a contradiction within the contract as the original understanding of available remedies has been fundamentally altered, leading to an in-text contradiction.
